---
title: "Exploring Penguin Species"
format: dashboard
editor: source
theme: 
    - yeti
---

```{r}
#| label: load-packages
#| message: false
#| warning: false
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
```


```{r}
#| label: load-data
#| message: false
#| warning: false
data("penguins")
#if the data is saved in a CSVfile
# data <- read_csv("datafilename.csv")
```


# Overview

## Row {height="20%"}

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Total births"

list(
  icon = "file-medical",
  color = "primary",
  value = 25
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Cesarean deliveries"

list(
  icon = "clipboard2-pulse",
  color = "red",
  value = 5
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Pre-term births"

list(
  icon = "calendar-week",
  color = "blue",
  value = 20
)
```

## Row {height="40%"}

### Column {width="40%"}

```{r}
ggplot(penguins, aes(x = species, fill=species)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = "How many penguins of each species?",
       x = "Species", y = "Count")
```

### Column {width="60%"}


```{r}
penguins |>
  ggplot(aes(x = island, fill = species)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Counts by island and species (side-by-side)",
       x = "Island", y = "Count")
```

# Body Size Relationships

# Gender-wise Comparison

# Species-wise Comparison
